questionText: Provide an indicative percentage split for production, 1P, 2P, 3P reserves, and total resource base by development types.
questionKo: an indicative percentage split for production, 1P, 2P, 3P reserves, 및 total resource base by development types.을/를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: The transition to a low-carbon economy may affect the value of reserves or long-lived assets. A breakdown of reserves and resource base by development type provides insight into potential future emissions. This insight can help to inform investors about the potential impacts of regulatory measures and demand changes on earning capacity. Information regarding the breakdown of conventional and unconventional hydrocarbons of the total resource base is valuable to investors.

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Upstream" in response to 1.19.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Upstream" in response to 1.19.

guidance.text:
Development type (column 1)
Select the development type for which you are providing an indicative percentage split for production, 1P, 2P, 3P reserves, and total resource base.
You are requested to provide the split for production, reserves and resources by development types. Data users are interested in development types in more granularity than conventional and unconventional development type.
The onshore, shallow-water, deepwater, ultra-deepwater and arctic development type options are considered conventional. Conventional refers to conventional hydrocarbons (i.e. not extra heavy crude), conventional recovery methods (i.e. not hydraulic fracturing) or conventional reservoirs (i.e. good permeability).
The oil sand/extra heavy oil, tight/shale and LNG development type options are considered unconventional.
Explanation of development types
Onshore: Assets onshore
Shallow-water: Assets in water depth 1,500m
Arctic: Assets located inside the Arctic Circle - north of the 66 degrees north latitude.
Oil sand/extra heavy oil: Oil sands extraction by mining and in-situ methods and other assets that produce oil with an API gravity of less than 10°.
Tight/shale: Combines the following:
Shale oil and gas; produced from petroleum source rock by horizontal drilling and hydraulic fracturing. 
Tight oil and gas; Oil and gas produced from formations of low permeability requiring hydraulic fracturing. 
(N.B. this does not include oil shale (kerogen) which is mined and cooked out of the source rock by pyrolysis - this should be reported in the ‘Other' category)
LNG: Upstream assets with LNG (Liquified Natural Gas) processing onsite (or where gas is exported to liquefaction facilities nearby which are associated with the upstream asset.)
Other, please specify: Assets that cannot be classified in any of the above categories.
In any cases where an asset would otherwise be in an unconventional category, the unconventional category is to be given precedence.
If there are assets that cannot be classified in any of the above development types then select "Other, please specify". If you select "Other, please specify," provide a label for the development type.
In-year net production (%) (column 2)
Production activities, for example, include the lifting of oil and gas to the surface and gathering, treating, field processing (as in the case of processing gas to extract liquid hydrocarbons), and field storage.
The production function shall normally be regarded as terminating at the outlet valve on the lease or field production storage tank. If unusual physical or operational circumstances exist, it may be more appropriate to regard the production function as terminating at the first point at which oil, gas, or gas liquids are delivered to a main pipeline, a common carrier, a refinery, or a marine terminal.
Net proved reserves (1P) (%) (column 3)
Net proved reserves have a reasonable certainty of being produced (90% confidence if probabilistic methods are used, hence the term P90).
Net proved + probable reserves (2P) (%) (column 4)
This is proved reserves plus probable reserves (often referred to as P50). Probable reserves are additional reserves less certain to be recovered, and in sum with proved reserves there is a 50% chance that actual quantities produced will equal or exceed this estimate.
Net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P) (%) (column 5)
This is proved reserves plus probable reserves plus possible reserves. Possible reserves are less certain than probable. There is a 10% chance that actual quantities produced will equal or exceed 3P (hence the term P10).
Net total resource base (%) (column 6)
Net total resource base includes the total for reserves, contingent resources and prospective resources.
Comment (column 7) (optional)
Use the "Comment" column to define and explain your hydrocarbon accounting and reported production figure(s), especially if your organizational boundary for emissions accounting and hydrocarbon accounting differ.
guidance.textKo: Development type (열 1) Select the development type for which you are providing an indicative percentage split for production, 1P, 2P, 3P reserves, 및 total resource base. You are requested to provide the split for production, reserves 및 resources by development types. 데이터 사용자 are interested in development types in more granularity than conventional 및 unconventional development type. The onshore, shallow-water, deepwater, ultra-deepwater 및 arctic development type options are considered conventional. Conventional refers to conventional hydrocarbons (i.e. not extra heavy crude), conventional recovery methods (i.e. not hydraulic fracturing) 또는 conventional reservoirs (i.e. good permeability). The oil sand/extra heavy oil, tight/shale 및 LNG development type options are considered unconventional. 설명 of development types Onshore: 자산 onshore Shallow-water: 자산 in water depth 1,500m Arctic: 자산 located inside the Arctic Circle - north 의 66 degrees north latitude. Oil sand/extra heavy oil: Oil sands extraction by mining 및 in-situ methods 및 other 자산 that produce oil with an API gravity of 미만 10°. Tight/shale: Combines the following: Shale oil 및 gas; produced from petroleum source rock by horizontal drilling 및 hydraulic fracturing. Tight oil 및 gas; Oil 및 gas produced from formations of low permeability requiring hydraulic fracturing. (N.B. this does not include oil shale (kerogen) which is mined 및 cooked out 의 source rock by pyrolysis - this 해야 합니다 be 보고 in the ‘Other' category) LNG: Upstream 자산 with LNG (Liquified Natural Gas) 프로세스ing onsite (or 여기서 gas is exported to liquefaction facilities nearby which are associated with the upstream asset.) Other, 직접 입력: 자산 that cannot be classified in 하나 이상 의 above categories. In 하나 이상 cases 여기서 an asset would otherwise be in an unconventional category, the unconventional category is to be given precedence. 만약 there are 자산 that cannot be classified in 하나 이상 의 above development types then select "Other, 직접 입력". 만약 you select "Other, 직접 입력," provide a label 에 대한 development type. In-year net production (%) (열 2) Production activities, for example, include the lifting of oil 및 gas to the surface 및 gathering, treating, field 프로세스ing (as in the case of 프로세스ing gas to extract liquid hydrocarbons), 및 field storage. The production function shall normally be regarded as terminating at the outlet valve on the lease 또는 field production storage tank. 만약 unusual physical 또는 operational circumstances exist, it may be more appropriate to regard the production function as terminating at the first 점 at which oil, gas, 또는 gas liquids are delivered to a main pipeline, a common carrier, a refinery, 또는 a marine terminal. Net proved reserves (1P) (%) (열 3) Net proved reserves have a reasonable certainty of being produced (90% confidence 만약 probabilistic methods are used, hence the term P90). Net proved + probable reserves (2P) (%) (열 4) This is proved reserves plus probable reserves (often referred to as P50). Probable reserves are additional reserves less certain to be recovered, 및 in sum with proved reserves there is a 50% chance that actual quantities produced will equal 또는 exceed this estimate. Net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P) (%) (열 5) This is proved reserves plus probable reserves plus possible reserves. Possible reserves are less certain than probable. There is a 10% chance that actual quantities produced will equal 또는 exceed 3P (hence the term P10). Net total resource base (%) (열 6) Net total resource base includes the total for reserves, contingent resources 및 prospective resources. Comment (열 7) (optional) Use the "Comment" 열 to define 및 explain your hydrocarbon accounting 및 보고 production figure(s), especially 만약 your 조직경계 for 배출량 회계 및 hydrocarbon accounting differ.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed (excluding columns 'Net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P) (%) AND 'Net total resource base (%)'). Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 8/8 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개 (제외 열 'Net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P) (%) 그리고 'Net total resource base (%)'). 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 8/8점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per row in proportion to the number of rows disclosed (excluding both columns 'Net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P)' (%) AND 'Net total resource base (%)').
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 행별로 점수가 부여됩니다 in proportion to the number of 행 공개 (제외 both 열 'Net proved + probable + possible reserves (3P)' (%) 그리고 'Net total resource base (%)'). 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다.

simpleHelpKo: The transition to a low-carbon economy may affect the value of reserves 또는 long-lived 자산. A breakdown of reserves 및 resource base by development type provides insight into potential future emissions. This insight can help to inform investors about the potential impacts of regulatory measures 및 demand changes on earning capacity. Information regarding the breakdown of conventional 및 unconventional hydrocarbons 의 total resource base is valuable to investors.
